title: Triparental mating of Synechocystis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bbmk1","text":"Protocol for genetic manipulation of Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803 using the triparental mating method described in Thiel et al., 1988.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":37,"length":13},{"style":"italic","offset":119,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dmh4h","text":"Thiel T, Wolk CP: Conjugal Transfer of Plasmids to Cyanobacteria. Methods in Enzymology 1988, 153:232–243.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":18,"length":46},{"style":"bold","offset":94,"length":3},{"style":"italic","offset":66,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"184ia","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"40uvu","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"7fpsg","text":"Always work under sterile conditions, even when working with E. coli.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f04ed","text":"Plasmids are mobilized with very high efficiency; make sure not to plate too much cell material to ensure proper spacing of colonies.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Always work under sterile conditions, even when working with E. coli . Inoculate 50-100 mL BG11 with Synechocystis strain of choice. Grow for 7 days - culture should be in exponential phase.

The day before performing mating reaction, inoculate one over night culture each for strains carrying moblilizable plasmid, as well as the J53 strain carrying RP4. Prepare BG11 agar plates containing 5 % LB (no antibiotics) . Place sterile HATF filter on top of agar.

In the morning, fresh cultures are inoculated by dilution. 4.875 mL of LB (no antibiotics) are inoculated with 125 µL of each over night culture and incubated for two and a half hours at 37˚C . For RP4, 5 mL fresh culture is required for each mating step. 02:30:00
The cultures are centrifuged at RT and 2500 rpm for 8 min and each pellet is resuspended in 1 ml LB. 00:08:00
For each mating reaction, 1 mL RP4 is gently mixed with 1 mL of conjugative plasmid strain.
The mixtures are centrifuged at RT , 2500 rpm, for 5 minutes , resuspended in 100 µL LB and incubated at 30˚C for 1 hour (no shaking). 01:00:00
Depending on the cell density, add 0.9 or 1.8 mL of Synechocystis culture to each mating reaction, mix gently, centrifuge at RT and 2500 rpm for 5 min , resuspend in 30 µL BG11. 00:05:00
Spot 30 µL of mating mixture on top of HATF filter. Cover plates and incubate in light chamber for 24-48 hours.

Carefully resuspend dried cyanobacteria from HATF filter with 100 µL BG11. Plate mixture on BG 11 containing appropriate antibiotics. Note: Do not plate excessive amounts of cell material, as mobilization is very efficient and colonies should be sufficiently separated on the plate.. After 10-14 days, should colonies become visible.
Transfer colonies on agar plates containing twice the amount of antibiotics. Verify correct clones by colony PCR with universal BioBrick or NeoBrick prime
HATF filters (Millipore), ∅ = 5 cm LB media 2xBG11 Bacto Agar, 1.5 %
creator: Anna Behle
